See Note 13&#8212;Derivatives and Financial Instruments, for additional information.</span></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="12" style="padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:top"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">** Sales and other operating revenues are attributable to countries based on the location of the operations generating the revenues.</span></td></tr></table></div></ix:nonNumeric><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Contract-Related Assets and Liabilities </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, receivables from contracts with customers were $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableGross" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-368">9,250</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableGross" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-369">7,781</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively.  Significant noncustomer balances, such as buy/sell receivables and excise tax receivables, were excluded from these amounts. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Our contract-related assets also include payments we make to our marketing customers related to incentive programs.  An incentive payment is initially recognized as an asset and subsequently amortized as a reduction to revenue over the contract term, which generally ranges from <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-66" name="psx:CustomerContractsTerm" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="f-370">5</ix:nonNumeric> to <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-67" name="psx:CustomerContractsTerm" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="f-371">15</ix:nonNumeric> years.  At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, our asset balances related to such payments were $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerAssetGross" scale="6" id="f-372">852</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerAssetGross" scale="6" id="f-373">820</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Our contract liabilities primarily represent advances from our customers prior to product or service delivery.  At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, contract liabilities were $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiability" scale="6" id="f-374">210</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiability" scale="6" id="f-375">198</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million, respectively. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Remaining Performance Obligations </span></div></ix:continuation><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"><ix:continuation id="f-346-2">Most of our contracts with customers are spot contracts or term contracts with only variable consideration.  We do not disclose remaining performance obligations for these contracts as the expected duration is one year or less or because the variable consideration has been allocated entirely to an unsatisfied performance obligation.  We also have certain contracts in our Midstream segment that include minimum volume commitments with fixed pricing.  At March&#160;31, 2026, the remaining performance obligations related to these minimum volume commitment contracts amounted to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:RevenueRemainingPerformanceObligation" scale="6" id="f-376">819</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.  This amount excludes variable consideration and estimates of variable rate escalation clauses in our contracts with customers and is expected to be recognized through 2036, with a weighted average remaining life of <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-68" name="us-gaap:RevenueRemainingPerformanceObligationExpectedTimingOfSatisfactionPeriod1" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-377">four years</ix:nonNumeric> as of March&#160;31, 2026.</ix:continuation> </span></div><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="margin-bottom:2pt;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">8</span></div></div></div><div id="i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_46"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:42.75pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Note 4&#8212;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:AllowanceForCreditLossesTextBlock" id="f-378" continuedAt="f-378-1" escape="true">Credit Losses </ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-378-1"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">We are exposed to credit losses primarily through our sales of refined petroleum products, renewable fuels, renewable feedstocks, crude oil, NGL and natural gas.  We assess each counterparty&#8217;s ability to pay for the products we sell by conducting a credit review.  The credit review considers our expected billing exposure and timing for payment and the counterparty&#8217;s established credit rating or our assessment of the counterparty&#8217;s creditworthiness based on our analysis of their financial statements when a credit rating is not available.  We also consider contract terms and conditions, country and political risk and business strategy in our evaluation.  A credit limit is established for each counterparty based on the outcome of this review.  We may require collateralized asset support or a prepayment to mitigate credit risk.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">We monitor our ongoing credit exposure through active review of counterparty balances against contract terms and due dates.  Our activities include timely account reconciliations, dispute resolution and payment confirmations.  We may employ collection agencies and legal counsel to pursue recovery of defaulted receivables.  In addition, when events and circumstances arise that may affect certain counterparties&#8217; abilities to fulfill their obligations, we enhance our credit monitoring, and we may seek collateral to support some transactions or require prepayments from higher-risk counterparties.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, we reported $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-379">11,910</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-380">9,771</ix:nonFraction> million of accounts and notes receivable, respectively, net of allowances of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent" scale="6" id="f-381">66</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent" scale="6" id="f-382">68</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively.  Based on an aging analysis at March&#160;31, 2026, more than <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-2" decimals="2" name="psx:AccountsAndNotesReceivablePercentOutstandingLessThan60Days" scale="-2" id="f-383">95</ix:nonFraction>% of our accounts receivable were outstanding less than 60 days. </span><span style="color:#ee2724;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">We are also exposed to credit losses from off-balance sheet exposures, such as guarantees of joint venture debt and accounts receivable sold under a securitization facility, as well as standby letters of credit.  See Note 9&#8212;Debt, Note 11&#8212;Guarantees, and Note 12&#8212;Contingencies and Commitments, for additional information on these off-balance sheet exposures.</span></div></ix:continuation><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div id="i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_49"></div><div><span name="us-gaap:LIFOInventoryAmount" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-392">6,078</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LIFOInventoryAmount" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-393">4,461</ix:nonFraction> million at March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, respectively.  The estimated excess of current replacement cost over LIFO cost of inventories amounted to approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-2" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ExcessOfReplacementOrCurrentCostsOverStatedLIFOValue" scale="9" id="f-394">10.5</ix:nonFraction> billion and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-4" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ExcessOfReplacementOrCurrentCostsOverStatedLIFOValue" scale="9" id="f-395">3.6</ix:nonFraction> billion at March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, respectively.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Certain planned reductions in inventory that are not expected to be replaced by the end of the year cause liquidations of LIFO inventory values.  LIFO liquidations did not have a material impact on net income for the three months ended March&#160;31, 2026 and 2025.</span></div></ix:continuation><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="margin-bottom:2pt;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">9</span></div></div></div><div id="i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_52"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:42.75pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Note 6&#8212;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:InvestmentTextBlock" id="f-396" continuedAt="f-396-1" escape="true">Investments, Loans and Long-Term Receivables </ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-396-1" continuedAt="f-396-2"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Equity Investments</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Dakota Access, LLC (Dakota Access) and Energy Transfer Crude Oil Company, LLC (ETCO)</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In 2020, the trial court presiding over litigation brought by the Standing Rock Sioux Tribe (the Tribe) ordered the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) to prepare an Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) addressing environmental impacts from an easement allowing the passage of the Dakota Access Pipeline (DAPL) under Lake Oahe in North Dakota.  Later in 2020, the trial court vacated the easement, but operations have been allowed to continue while the USACE proceeds with the EIS as ordered.  The Tribe&#8217;s requests for a shutdown have been denied.  In March 2025, the trial court dismissed a second lawsuit filed by the Tribe, again challenging USACE&#8217;s allowance of pipeline operations while the EIS process proceeds.  The Tribe&#8217;s lawsuit was premature, and the trial court held that it cannot be refiled until after a final EIS is issued.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In December 2025, the USACE published its final EIS, completing its analysis of alternatives.  The final EIS evaluates five alternatives: two no-action alternatives (denial with restoration or abandonment) and three action alternatives, with one marked as USACE&#8217;s preferred alternative, that would grant the easement under varying conditions.  The preferred alternative would grant the easement subject to the same conditions as the 2017 easement but would authorize an increased throughput volume of 1.1&#160;million barrels per day (bpd), up from the previous 570,000 bpd under the original authorization.  The remaining action alternatives would impose either additional operational conditions or require an alternate pipeline route, both of which may entail substantial implementation costs and could have a material impact on our financial statements.   </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">We are still awaiting a Record of Decision (ROD), which will provide a definitive statement of the selected alternative and any related conditions.  The Tribe and affiliated parties may file a new lawsuit in Washington, D.C., challenging the ROD shortly after it is issued.  Most recently, the Tribe has appealed the dismissal, but the issuance of the ROD will supersede and effectively moot the appeal.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Dakota Access and ETCO have guaranteed repayment of senior unsecured notes issued by a wholly owned subsidiary of Dakota Access.  At March&#160;31, 2026, the aggregate principal amount outstanding of Dakota Access&#8217; senior unsecured notes was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-69" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" scale="6" id="f-397">850</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In addition, Phillips 66 Partners LP (Phillips 66 Partners), a wholly owned subsidiary of Phillips 66, and its co-venturers in Dakota Access also provided a Contingent Equity Contribution Undertaking (CECU) in conjunction with the notes offering.  Under the CECU, the co-venturers may be severally required to make proportionate equity contributions to Dakota Access if there is an unfavorable final judgment in the above-mentioned ongoing litigation.  At March&#160;31, 2026, our <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-70" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-398">25</ix:nonFraction>% share of the maximum potential equity contributions under the CECU was approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-70" decimals="-6" name="psx:MaximumExposureUndiscounted" scale="6" id="f-399">215</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.  If the pipeline is required to cease operations, it may have a material adverse effect on our results of operations and cash flows.  Should operations cease and Dakota Access and ETCO not have sufficient funds to pay its expenses, we also could be required to support our <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-70" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-400">25</ix:nonFraction>% share of the ongoing expenses, including scheduled interest payments on the notes of approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-71" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentPeriodicPaymentInterest" scale="6" id="f-401">10</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million annually, in addition to the potential obligations under the CECU at March&#160;31, 2026.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">At March&#160;31, 2026, the aggregate book value of our investments in Dakota Access and ETCO was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-70" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestments" scale="6" id="f-402">842</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">OnCue Holdings, LLC (OnCue)</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">We hold a <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-72" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-403">50</ix:nonFraction>% interest in OnCue, a joint venture that owns and operates retail convenience stores.  We fully guarantee various debt agreements of OnCue, and our co-venturer does not participate in the guarantees.  This entity is considered a variable interest entity (VIE) because our debt agreements resulted in OnCue not being exposed to all potential losses.  We have determined that we are not the primary beneficiary because we do not have the power to direct the activities that most significantly impact economic performance.  At March&#160;31, 2026, our maximum exposure to loss was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-72" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:VariableInterestEntityEntityMaximumLossExposureAmount" scale="6" id="f-404">271</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million, which represented the book value of our investment in OnCue of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-72" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestments" scale="6" id="f-405">218</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million and guaranteed debt obligations of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-72" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:GuaranteeObligationsMaximumExposure" scale="6" id="f-406">53</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. </span></div></ix:continuation><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div our Los Angeles Refinery and ceased fuel production.  We have submitted redevelopment project applications for the facilities, initiating the review process and allowing us to continue pursuing the redevelopment of the property for future uses.  As a result of the decision to cease operations and begin idling the facilities, the carrying values of the associated net properties, plants and equipment (PP&amp;E) and intangible assets were depreciated to the estimated salvage value of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-92" decimals="-6" name="psx:PropertiesPlantsAndEquipmentNetAndIntangibleAssetsNetSalvageValue" scale="6" id="f-460">241</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million at December 31, 2025.  Total depreciation related to the Los Angeles Refinery assets for the three months ended March&#160;31, 2025, was $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-93" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization" scale="6" id="f-461">270</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million, including $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-93" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:Depreciation" scale="6" id="f-462">246</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of accelerated depreciation, and was included within the &#8220;Depreciation and amortization&#8221; line item on our consolidated statement of income reported in our Refining segment.  In the first quarter of 2026, we transferred  $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-94" decimals="-6" name="psx:PropertyPlantAndEquipmentAndFinanceLeaseRightOfUseAssetBeforeAccumulatedDepreciationAndAmortizationTransfers" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-463">2,965</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million in gross PP&amp;E and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-94" decimals="-6" name="psx:PropertyPlantAndEquipmentAndFinanceLeaseRightOfUseAssetAccumulatedDepreciationAndAmortizationTransfers" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="6" id="f-464">2,699</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of accumulated depreciation and amortization associated with the idled Los Angeles Refinery from our Refining segment to Corporate and Other.</ix:continuation> </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="margin-bottom:2pt;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">11</span></div></div></div><div id="i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_64"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:42.75pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:112%">Note 8&#8212;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerShareTextBlock" id="f-467" continuedAt="f-467-1" escape="true">Earnings Per Share </ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-467-1"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerSharePolicyTextBlock" id="f-468" escape="true"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The numerator of basic earnings per share (EPS) is net income attributable to Phillips 66, adjusted for noncancelable dividends paid on unvested share-based employee awards during the vesting period (participating securities).  The denominator of basic EPS is the sum of the daily weighted-average number of common shares outstanding during the periods presented and fully vested stock and unit awards that have not yet been issued as common stock.  The numerator of diluted EPS is also based on net income attributable to Phillips 66, which is reduced by dividend equivalents paid on participating securities for which the dividends are more dilutive than the participation of the awards in the earnings of the periods presented.  To the extent unvested stock, unit or option awards and vested unexercised stock options are dilutive, they are included with the weighted-average common shares outstanding in the denominator.  Treasury stock is excluded from the denominator in both basic and diluted EPS.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfEarningsPerShareBasicAndDilutedTableTextBlock" id="f-469" escape="true"><div style="margin-bottom:1pt"><table style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Senior Notes and Term Loan Issuances and Repayments</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Repayments</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On February 17, 2026, upon maturity, Phillips 66 repaid the remaining $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-96" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:RepaymentsOfDebt" scale="6" id="f-499">100</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million outstanding on its <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-97" decimals="5" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-500">1.300</ix:nonFraction>% Senior Notes due February 2026, with an aggregate principal amount of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-97" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" scale="6" id="f-501">500</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On February 18, 2025, upon maturity, Phillips 66 Partners repaid its <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-98" decimals="5" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-502">3.605</ix:nonFraction>% Senior Notes due February 2025, with an aggregate principal amount of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-99" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:RepaymentsOfDebt" scale="6" id="f-503">59</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Term Loan Agreement</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On March 18, 2026 (the Term Loan Closing Date), Phillips 66 Company entered into a <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-100" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-504">364-day</ix:nonNumeric>, $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-101" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-505">2.25</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion term loan agreement guaranteed by Phillips 66 (the Term Loan Agreement).  The Term Loan Agreement provides for a single borrowing on the Term Loan Closing Date and matures <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-100" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm" format="ixt-sec:durday" id="f-506">364</ix:nonNumeric> days after the Term Loan Closing Date.  The Term Loan Agreement contains customary covenants similar to those contained in our revolving credit agreement, including a maximum consolidated net debt-to-capitalization ratio of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-100" decimals="2" name="psx:DebtInstrumentCovenantDebtToCapitalizationRatio" scale="-2" id="f-507">65</ix:nonFraction>% as of the last day of each fiscal quarter.  The Term Loan Agreement has customary events of default, such as nonpayment of principal when due; nonpayment of interest, fees or other amounts after grace periods; and violation of covenants.  We may at any time prepay outstanding borrowings under the Term Loan Agreement, in whole or in part, without premium or penalty.  Outstanding borrowings under the Term Loan Agreement bear interest at either: (a) the term Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) in effect from time to time plus an applicable margin of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-102" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentBasisSpreadOnVariableRate1" scale="-2" id="f-508">1.100</ix:nonFraction>%; or (b) the reference rate (as described in the Term Loan Agreement) plus an applicable margin of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-103" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentBasisSpreadOnVariableRate1" scale="-2" id="f-509">0.100</ix:nonFraction>%.  At March&#160;31, 2026, the entire $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-104" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount" scale="9" id="f-510">2.25</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion was borrowed under the Term Loan Agreement, which matures in March 2027.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Accounts Receivable Securitization</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On September 30, 2024, Phillips 66 Company entered into a <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-105" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-511">364-day</ix:nonNumeric>, $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-106" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="6" id="f-512">500</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million accounts receivable securitization facility (the Receivables Securitization Facility).  Under the Receivables Securitization Facility, Phillips 66 Company sells or contributes on an ongoing basis, certain of its receivables, together with related security and interests in the proceeds thereof, to its wholly owned subsidiary, Phillips 66 Receivables LLC (P66 Receivables), a consolidated and bankruptcy-remote special purpose entity created for the sole purpose of transacting under the Receivables Securitization Facility.  During 2025, Phillips 66 Company amended the Receivables Securitization Facility to, among other things, increase the maximum size of the Receivables Securitization Facility to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-107" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-513">1.25</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion and extend the term of the facility through September 28, 2026.  On March 13, 2026, Phillips 66 Company amended the Receivables Securitization Facility to, among other things, increase the maximum size of the Receivables Securitization Facility from $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-108" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-514">1.25</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-109" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-515">1.75</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion and permit P66 Receivables to request a future increase in the maximum facility size to up to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-110" decimals="-8" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityAccordionFeatureIncreaseLimit" scale="9" id="f-516">2.0</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion.  Under the amended Receivables Securitization Facility, P66 Receivables may borrow and incur indebtedness from, and/or sell certain accounts receivable in an amount not to exceed $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-109" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-517">1.75</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion in the aggregate, and will secure its obligations with a pledge of undivided interests in such receivables, together with related security and interests in the proceeds thereof, to PNC Bank, National Association, as Administrative Agent, for the benefit of the secured parties thereunder.  Accounts receivable outstanding under the Receivables Securitization Facility accrue interest at an adjusted term SOFR plus the applicable margin.  In all instances, Phillips 66 Company retains the servicing of the accounts receivables transferred.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Accounts receivable sold under the Receivables Securitization Facility meet the sale criteria under ASC 860, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Transfers and Servicing,</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and are derecognized from the consolidated balance sheet.  P66 Receivables guarantees payment, in full, for accounts receivable sold to the purchasers.  For the three months ended March 31, 2026, we sold $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-111" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ConsiderationReceivedForBeneficialInterestObtainedForTransferringFinancialAsset" scale="6" id="f-518">264</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of accounts receivable in exchange for a $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-112" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ConsiderationReceivedForBeneficialInterestObtainedForTransferringFinancialAsset" scale="6" id="f-519">264</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million reduction in our borrowings under the Receivables Securitization Facility, which was recognized as a non-cash financing transaction.  For the three months ended March 31, 2025, we sold $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-113" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromSaleOfFinanceReceivables" scale="6" id="f-520">130</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of accounts receivables for cash proceeds under the Receivables Securitization Facility.  We recognized immaterial charges associated with the transfers of financial assets, which are included as a component within the line item &#8220;Selling, general and administrative expense&#8221; on our consolidated statement of income, during the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025. </span></div></ix:continuation><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="margin-bottom:2pt;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">13</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:42.75pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-498-2" continuedAt="f-498-3"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Borrowings under the Receivables Securitization Facility are recognized as short-term debt on the consolidated balance sheet.  Borrowings are secured by the accounts receivable, held by P66 Receivables, which remain reported as accounts receivable on the consolidated balance sheet.  At March 31, 2026, and December 31, 2025, we had outstanding borrowings of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-114" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount" scale="6" id="f-521">384</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-115" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount" scale="6" id="f-522">200</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million, respectively.  These borrowings were secured by accounts receivable held by P66 Receivables of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-116" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:TradeReceivablesHeldForSaleAmount" scale="9" id="f-523">5.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-117" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:TradeReceivablesHeldForSaleAmount" scale="9" id="f-524">4.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion as of March 31, 2026, and December 31, 2025, respectively, which are included within the &#8220;Accounts and notes receivable&#8221; line item on our consolidated balance sheet.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">At March 31, 2026, we had utilized $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-118" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumMonthendOutstandingAmount" scale="6" id="f-525">650</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of the $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-110" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-526">1.75</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion capacity of the Receivables Securitization Facility from $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-110" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:TransferOfFinancialAssetsAccountedForAsSalesAmountDerecognized" scale="6" id="f-527">266</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of sold accounts receivable not yet remitted to the Administrative Agent and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-119" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount" scale="6" id="f-528">384</ix:nonFraction> million of outstanding borrowings.   At December 31, 2025, we had utilized $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-120" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumMonthendOutstandingAmount" scale="6" id="f-529">367</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of the $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-107" decimals="-7" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-530">1.25</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion capacity of the Receivables Securitization Facility from $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-107" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:TransferOfFinancialAssetsAccountedForAsSalesAmountDerecognized" scale="6" id="f-531">167</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of sold accounts receivable not yet remitted to the Administrative Agent and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-121" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount" scale="6" id="f-532">200</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million of outstanding borrowings.  </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Credit Facilities and Commercial Paper</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Phillips 66 and Phillips 66 Company</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On January 13, 2025, we entered into a $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-122" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="6" id="f-533">200</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million uncommitted credit facility (the 2025 Uncommitted Facility) with Phillips 66 Company as the borrower and Phillips 66 as the guarantor.  The 2025 Uncommitted Facility contains covenants and events of default customary for unsecured uncommitted facilities.  The 2025 Uncommitted Facility has <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-122" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:CompensatingBalanceAmount" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-534"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-123" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityCommitmentFeeAmount" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-535">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> commitment fees or compensating balance requirements.  Outstanding borrowings under the 2025 Uncommitted Facility bear interest at a rate of either (a) the adjusted term SOFR plus the applicable margin, (b) the adjusted daily simple SOFR plus the applicable margin or (c) the base rate, in each case plus the applicable margin.  Each borrowing matures <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-123" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityBorrowingMaturityPeriod" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-536">six months</ix:nonNumeric> from the date of such borrowing.  We may at any time prepay outstanding borrowings, in whole or in part, without premium or penalty.  On March 26, 2026, Phillips 66 amended the 2025 Uncommitted Facility to increase the capacity by $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-124" decimals="-6" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacityIncrease" scale="6" id="f-537">100</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million.  At March&#160;31, 2026, the entire $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-125" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" scale="6" id="f-538">300</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million was outstanding, while at December 31, 2025, <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-126" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="6" id="f-539">no</ix:nonFraction> borrowings were outstanding under the 2025 Uncommitted Facility.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On June 25, 2024, we entered into a $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-127" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="6" id="f-540">400</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million uncommitted credit facility (the 2024 Uncommitted Facility) with Phillips 66 Company as the borrower and Phillips 66 as the guarantor.  The 2024 Uncommitted Facility contains covenants and events of default customary for unsecured uncommitted facilities.  The 2024 Uncommitted Facility has <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-128" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityCommitmentFeeAmount" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-541"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-127" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:CompensatingBalanceAmount" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-542">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> commitment fees or compensating balance requirements.  Outstanding borrowings under the 2024 Uncommitted Facility bear interest at a rate of either (a) the adjusted term SOFR, (b) the adjusted daily simple SOFR or (c) the reference rate, in each case plus the applicable margin.  Each borrowing matures <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-128" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityBorrowingMaturityPeriod" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-543">six months</ix:nonNumeric> from the date of such borrowing.  We may at any time prepay outstanding borrowings, in whole or in part, without premium or penalty.  At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-129" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="6" id="f-544"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-130" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="6" id="f-545">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> borrowings were outstanding under the 2024 Uncommitted Facility.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On February 28, 2024, we entered into a new $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-131" decimals="-9" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-546">5</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion revolving credit agreement (the Facility) with Phillips 66 Company as the borrower and Phillips 66 as the guarantor and a scheduled maturity date of February 28, 2029.  The Facility replaced our previous $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-132" decimals="-9" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-547">5</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion revolving credit facility dated as of June 23, 2022, with Phillips 66 Company as the borrower and Phillips 66 as the guarantor, and the previous revolving credit facility was terminated.  The Facility contains customary covenants similar to the previous revolving credit facility, including a maximum consolidated net debt-to-capitalization ratio of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-133" decimals="2" name="psx:DebtInstrumentCovenantDebtToCapitalizationRatio" scale="-2" id="f-548">65</ix:nonFraction>% as of the last day of each fiscal quarter.  The Facility has customary events of default, such as nonpayment of principal when due; nonpayment of interest, fees or other amounts after grace periods; and violation of covenants. We may at any time prepay outstanding borrowings under the Facility, in whole or in part, without premium or penalty.  We have the option to increase the overall capacity to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-131" decimals="-9" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityAccordionFeatureIncreaseLimit" scale="9" id="f-549">6</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion, subject to certain conditions.  We also have the option to extend the scheduled maturity of the Facility for up to <ix:nonFraction unitRef="option" contextRef="c-131" decimals="INF" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityNumberOfOptionsToExtend" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-550">two</ix:nonFraction> additional <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-133" name="psx:LineOfCreditFacilityExtensionTerm" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="f-551">one-year</ix:nonNumeric> terms, subject to, among other things, the consent of the lenders holding the majority of the commitments and of each lender extending its commitment.  Outstanding borrowings under the Facility bear interest at either: (a) the adjusted term SOFR (as described in the Facility) in effect from time to time plus the applicable margin; or (b) the reference rate (as described in the Facility) plus the applicable margin.  The pricing levels for the commitment fee and interest-rate margins are determined based on the ratings in effect for our senior unsecured long-term debt from time to time.  At March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025, <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-134" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-552"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-135" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-553">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> amount had been drawn under the Facility.</span></div></ix:continuation><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:42.75pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="margin-bottom:2pt;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">14</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:42.75pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i35f3571d4c894d20a65545fd3d353a77_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-498-3"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Phillips 66 also has a $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-136" decimals="-9" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="9" id="f-554">5</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion uncommitted commercial paper program for short-term working capital needs that is supported by the Facility. In addition, unless otherwise stated, we are not currently performing with any significance under the guarantees and expect future performance to be either immaterial or have only a remote chance of occurrence.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Lease Residual Value Guarantees</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10.5pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Under the operating lease agreement for our headquarters facility in Houston, Texas, we have the option, at the end of the lease term in September 2030, to request to renew the lease, purchase the facility or assist the lessor in marketing it for resale.
